vibe: Saturday slowburn — deep house warmth, city pop shimmer, lo-fi jazz, Brazilian funk, reggaeton grit, UK jazz grooves, and math rock edges

tracks.name: Post Tenebras Lux
tracks.reason: Folamour does that warm, euphoric deep house that sits right next to &ME and Jimpster in your rotation — proper Saturday morning opener energy.

tracks.name: Midnight Pretenders
tracks.reason: Deep cut city pop from '83 — same dreamy synth-funk universe as Taeko Onuki and 加藤有紀 but with this gorgeous nocturnal edge you haven't heard yet.

tracks.name: Soul Of Freedom feat. Cise Starr
tracks.reason: Uyama Hiroto was Nujabes' closest collaborator — this is jazzy lo-fi with Cise Starr's flow on top. If you love City Lights, this is the next stop.

tracks.name: Pa' Que Retozen
tracks.reason: Classic Tego — raw, grimy reggaeton from before it went pop. If Bad Bunny is in your top artists, this is the godfather track you need in the collection.

tracks.name: Estrelar
tracks.reason: Brazilian boogie-funk from '83 that sounds like it was produced yesterday. Same soul-meets-synth energy as the Sade and Grover Washington Jr. you've been spinning.

tracks.name: FUNKEE MAH－CHAN
tracks.reason: Takanaka is the Japanese guitar funk wizard — think Soichi Terada's cousin who plays guitar instead of synths. Pure tropical funk heat.

tracks.name: Chasing the Drum
tracks.reason: You've got Tom Misch in your recents — Yussef Dayes is his collab partner gone solo with this hypnotic UK jazz percussion piece that builds and builds.

tracks.name: Glue
tracks.reason: Nostalgic rave electronica that lives in the same emotional space as your New Order obsession — melancholic synths over a driving beat. Essential.

tracks.name: Reason in Disguise
tracks.reason: Ezra Collective with Jorja Smith is UK jazz at its most gorgeous — Mercury Prize winners doing what Nujabes did but with a London twist. The horns are ridiculous.

tracks.name: potage
tracks.reason: Japanese math rock that hits like a puzzle you enjoy solving — intricate guitar work and time signature changes that keep your brain engaged. Perfect Tokyo soundtrack.
